task_completion.description: Load the survey data into TopoCal
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the TopoCal application open with point cloud or survey data loaded and visible in the main workspace.
Look for a triangulated irregular network (TIN) or mesh surface (MDT) generated and displayed over the points in the TopoCal workspace.

task_completion.description: Determine interpolated elevation for NE and NW coordinates
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for TopoCal's coordinate readout, cursor tooltip, or query tool displaying X, Y coordinates near (5050.00, 5050.00) for NE and (4950.00, 5050.00) for NW, along with their corresponding Z elevation values.

task_completion.description: Determine interpolated elevation for SW and SE coordinates
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for TopoCal's coordinate readout, cursor tooltip, or query tool displaying X, Y coordinates near (4950.00, 4950.00) for SW and (5050.00, 4950.00) for SE, along with their corresponding Z elevation values.

task_completion.description: Create the target text file for results
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor (e.g., Notepad) open and saving a file specifically named 'building_pad_elevations.txt' in the C:\Users\Docker\Documents\ directory.

task_completion.description: Save the queried results in the specified format
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the text editor to verify it contains four lines of data, formatted exactly as 'CornerLabel, X, Y, Z' (e.g., 'NE, 5050.00, 5050.00, 123.45').

integrity.description: Agent used the TopoCal GUI to perform the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent actively clicking through TopoCal menus and using its tools to build the MDT and query points, rather than using command-line tools.

integrity.description: Z-values in the text file match the TopoCal queries
integrity.visual_evidence: The Z-values typed into the text file should correspond to the Z-values seen in the TopoCal interface during the coordinate querying steps, proving they were not hallucinated.

integrity.description: Agent did not use a script to bypass the application
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal or IDE windows showing Python or PowerShell scripts written to automatically generate the output file with fabricated data.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected output file: C:\Users\Docker\Documents\building_pad_elevations.txt. Tolerances: XY = 1.5, Z = 2.5. Note: The task environment is critically defective and lacks the 'Colorado foothills' survey data file, making it impossible to verify exact Z values. The VLM should verify that the agent attempted the workflow (loading data, creating an MDT, querying the specified X/Y coordinates, and formatting the output file) using the TopoCal GUI, accepting whatever Z-values the agent obtains or reasonably substitutes during the process.
